what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon showing multiple devices: a larger screen with a small stand/base and a smaller overlapping upright device.
observations: Root SVG uses viewBox "0 0 24 24" with fill set to none and stroke set to currentColor.
Line styling uses stroke-width 1.5 with round caps and round joins.
The icon is built from two rounded rectangles and one path.
The first rectangle spans x=3 y=6 width=13 height=9 with rx=1.
The second rectangle spans x=14 y=9 width=7 height=11 with rx=1.
The path draws a horizontal base segment and a vertical support under the larger screen.
standouts: Single inline SVG expresses a multi-device concept with minimal primitives.
Uses currentColor so the icon inherits text or parent color.
conventions: Follows a common 24x24 outline icon format.
Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for softened line endpoints.
Contains no IDs, classes, embedded styles, or script.
concerns: No title or desc metadata is present for standalone accessibility context.
notes: A compact currentColor SVG icon for representing multiple devices or responsive display contexts.
questions: Should this asset be treated as decorative by default or given an accessible label at the call site?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a multi-device icon, and the SVG content draws overlapping device outlines, so the apparent purpose matches the actual content.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable design-system icon for device plurality or responsive layout contexts.
novelty: The composition combines a desktop-like screen stand with a tall overlapping device in a very small set of SVG primitives.
contribution: Contributes a neutral, theme-inheriting multi-device outline glyph that a fused system could keep as a reusable visual token for responsive or device-related UI states.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon made from two rounded rectangles and a stand path to depict multiple devices.
intention_for_embedding: It was likely created as a reusable design-system icon for representing responsive design, device previews, or multi-device support.
